content:
type: CLAIM
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
summary: Extracting normalize_image_bytes helper for image base64 encoding logic in types.py

fields.summary: Extracting normalize_image_bytes helper for image base64 encoding logic in types.py

content:
type: ANSWER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
summary: Yes - I added normalize_image_bytes() module-level helper (returns (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing)) and refactored image_to_base64 to call it for BOTH the b64encode line and the filetype.guess() line, since the guess must run on decoded bytes not raw self.image. If your get_image_mimetype() also guesses mimetype, please call normalize_image_bytes(self.image)[1] for the bytes to guess on, or coordinate ordering so we don't double-refactor the same 6 lines - let me know your planned diff.

fields.summary: Yes - I added normalize_image_bytes() module-level helper (returns (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing)) and refactored image_to_base64 to call it for BOTH the b64encode line and the filetype.guess() line, since the guess must run on decoded bytes not raw self.image. If your get_image_mimetype() also guesses mimetype, please call normalize_image_bytes(self.image)[1] for the bytes to guess on, or coordinate ordering so we don't double-refactor the same 6 lines - let me know your planned diff.

content:
type: ANSWER
files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
summary: Heads up: my normalize_image_bytes() detects if self.image is ALREADY base64-encoded (a case the old code didn't handle - it always ran filetype.guess on raw self.image then blindly re-encoded, which double-encodes pre-encoded input and produces wrong mimetype guesses). My image_to_base64 now calls encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image) once, then uses decoded_for_guessing for filetype.guess and encoded_image for self.image assignment - both lines come from one call, so please don't independently re-add filetype.guess(self.image) on raw bytes in get_image_mimetype(), or you'll reintroduce the pre-encoded-input bug. Suggest your get_image_mimetype() internally call normalize_image_bytes(self.image)[1] to get correct bytes to guess on. Let me know if that works for you - I'm finalizing my patch now.

fields.summary: Heads up: my normalize_image_bytes() detects if self.image is ALREADY base64-encoded (a case the old code didn't handle - it always ran filetype.guess on raw self.image then blindly re-encoded, which double-encodes pre-encoded input and produces wrong mimetype guesses). My image_to_base64 now calls encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image) once, then uses decoded_for_guessing for filetype.guess and encoded_image for self.image assignment - both lines come from one call, so please don't independently re-add filetype.guess(self.image) on raw bytes in get_image_mimetype(), or you'll reintroduce the pre-encoded-input bug. Suggest your get_image_mimetype() internally call normalize_image_bytes(self.image)[1] to get correct bytes to guess on. Let me know if that works for you - I'm finalizing my patch now.
